commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b7c49b85568bd4f598eb0a9b14aac4cdf50c3366)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core so QMD-backed `memory_search` can use manager-reported whole-search deadlines, bounds search/sync/cleanup waits, and extends `MemorySearchManager` with optional timeout and close parameters.

PR surface: Source +293, Tests +357. Total +650 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and the latest release still use the fixed 15s `memory_search` wrapper while QMD config/docs expose longer `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs`, and CI/source inspection reproduce a PR-introduced unbound-method failure.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor code and proof updates plus maintainer API/availability judgment; missing real behavior proof prevents routing this as an automated repair marker.

target.title: fix(memory): honor qmd search timeout and bound one-shot CLI cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

- Honors configured QMD search timeout instead of forcing QMD-backed `memory_search` through the historical 15s outer guard.
- Keeps the default `memory_search` deadline at 15s unless the active QMD manager reports a longer whole-search budget.
- Preserves the rebased QMD `AbortSignal` subprocess-cancellation behavior from PR #93394.
- Bounds one-shot CLI QMD manager cleanup so pending update work cannot hold tool return indefinitely.

## Real behavior proof expected by ClawSweeper

ClawSweeper is looking for behavior proof, not only implementation-shape proof. The important proof is a terminal run showing that a real QMD-backed delayed search survives past the old 15s wrapper and returns a QMD memory result after this PR.

The proof should establish:

1. Before this change, the fixed 15s `memory_search` guard would return an unavailable timeout result.
2. After this change, a QMD manager with a longer configured search budget can continue past 15s.
3. The delayed QMD-backed search returns an actual memory hit instead of timing out.
4. The rebase still preserves QMD subprocess cancellation from PR #93394.

## Behavior covered by tests

- `keeps qmd manager setup on the default memory_search deadline`: default managers still use the historical 15s guard.
- `uses the qmd manager whole-search budget for slow memory searches`: QMD-backed searches can use the manager-reported longer lifecycle budget.
- `keeps builtin forced-sync retry inside the original memory_search deadline`: built-in retry does not escape the original tool lifecycle.
- `keeps qmd zero-hit forced-sync retry inside one whole-search deadline`: QMD search, forced sync, and retry share one bounded budget.
- `closes one-shot CLI qmd manager with timeout when forced sync never settles`: one-shot cleanup is bounded.
- `keeps the timeout result when an abort-aware search rejects on abort`: timeout returns a stable unavailable payload.
- QMD abort coverage: direct search, grouped search, collection probe, mcporter search, and already-aborted paths all preserve caller-signal cancellation.
- QMD debug coverage: zero-hit retry debug merge, structured QMD debug metadata, manager acquisition timing, and cache-state payloads are preserved.

## Validation commands

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "timeout|abort|close"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
git diff --check
```

## Latest local validation status

Confirmed passing locally:

- `search-manager.test.ts`: 37 passed.
- `qmd-manager.test.ts -t "timeout|abort|close"`: 17 passed, 133 skipped.
- `qmd-process.test.ts`: 20 passed.

Still needs clean final rerun before merge:

- `tools.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes for reviewers

The main rebase risk was losing QMD abort propagation that had already landed on `main`. This PR is intended to preserve that cancellation behavior while adding the QMD whole-search budget and bounded one-shot CLI cleanup.

The availability tradeoff is intentional: if a user configures a longer QMD timeout, interactive `memory_search` can now wait longer than the old fixed 15s wrapper instead of failing early.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix(memory): honor qmd search timeout and bound one-shot CLI cleanup

- Keeps the default `memory_search` deadline at 15s unless the active QMD manager reports a longer whole-search budget.
- Preserves the rebased QMD `AbortSignal` subprocess-cancellation behavior from PR #93394.
- Bounds one-shot CLI QMD manager cleanup so pending update work cannot hold tool return indefinitely.

## Real behavior proof expected by ClawSweeper

ClawSweeper is looking for behavior proof, not only implementation-shape proof. The important proof is a terminal run showing that a real QMD-backed delayed search survives past the old 15s wrapper and returns a QMD memory result after this PR.

The proof should establish:

1. Before this change, the fixed 15s `memory_search` guard would return an unavailable timeout result.
2. After this change, a QMD manager with a longer configured search budget can continue past 15s.
3. The delayed QMD-backed search returns an actual memory hit instead of timing out.
4. The rebase still preserves QMD subprocess cancellation from PR #93394.

## Behavior covered by tests

- `keeps qmd manager setup on the default memory_search deadline`: default managers still use the historical 15s guard.
- `uses the qmd manager whole-search budget for slow memory searches`: QMD-backed searches can use the manager-reported longer lifecycle budget.
- `keeps builtin forced-sync retry inside the original memory_search deadline`: built-in retry does not escape the original tool lifecycle.
- `keeps qmd zero-hit forced-sync retry inside one whole-search deadline`: QMD search, forced sync, and retry share one bounded budget.
- `closes one-shot CLI qmd manager with timeout when forced sync never settles`: one-shot cleanup is bounded.
- `keeps the timeout result when an abort-aware search rejects on abort`: timeout returns a stable unavailable payload.

- QMD debug coverage: zero-hit retry debug merge, structured QMD debug metadata, manager acquisition timing, and cache-state payloads are preserved.

## Validation commands

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "timeout|abort|close"
```

- `tools.test.ts`
